task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 12 Use of the Service media profiles

abstract.p: This document describes the use of the Service media profiles.

section.title: Use of the Service media profiles
section.p: The system makes use of media profiles that define media and printer properties the printer needs to know in order to assure best possible print results.
The media profiles are defined in the RIP and made available to the printer via an interface.

For system installation but also for troubleshooting, we want to be independent from the RIP settings. In order to do engine calibrations and test prints without the need of an external RIP, 3 service media have been defined that can be selected during media loading:
The content of these profiles cannot be changed by any means. Hence it is assured that on all printers these settings are consistently the same for troubleshooting.
The Service tests profiles are not intended to be used by the customer for regular printing.

section.title: Persistent storage of the media thickness
section.p: Since the actual installation media (and thus also its thickness) might be different from country to country, the actual media thickness of the installation media needs to be measured on-site and entered in SDS.
3 SDS parameters are available to set the media thickness of the Service media:

li.p: SDS 25-1-004 Media thickness of Service tests - Vinyl media [um]
SDS 25-1-005 Media thickness of Service tests - Film media [um]
SDS 25-1-006 Media thickness of Service tests - Paper media [um]

section.p: In order to use a Service media, the parameter must be set accordingly.
The parameter is used to determine the initial print gap (carriage height) that is required for this media. Also the value of this parameter is used as a starting point for calibrations.
